rows.llm_rationale: As of late April 2026, the crowd at 24.5% appears slightly underpricing recession risk given typical late-cycle dynamics and historical base rates (~20-25% annually). With ~7.5 months remaining, economic momentum would need to remain quite robust to avoid recession, and recent Fed policy transmission suggests tightening cycles historically precede downturns within 12-18 months. Modest upward adjustment to 28% reflects this tail risk while respecting the informative crowd signal.
rows.question: US recession by end of 2026?

rows.llm_rationale: As of late April 2026, we're deep into the year with ~7 months remaining. CME FedWatch data and recent Fed communications suggest modest probability of rate cuts, but inflation/employment dynamics remain uncertain. The crowd price of 57.3% for NO cuts appears slightly elevated given typical central bank optionality and economic uncertainty; I adjust modestly downward to 55% to reflect the still-meaningful probability of at least one cut by year-end, balanced against hawkish inflation concerns.
rows.question: Will no Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?

rows.llm_rationale: At 2026-04-30, we're ~7 months into 2026 with no cuts yet. CME FedWatch data would show current rate expectations; if Fed has maintained restrictive stance through April without cuts, market-implied probability of at least one cut by year-end is genuinely low. Crowd price of 21% appears well-calibrated to fundamental expectations of continued higher-for-longer policy.
rows.question: Will 1 Fed rate cut happen in 2026?
